text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Medical Oncology — Genitourinary Oncology Service

ATTENDING: [Attending Oncologist]
FELLOW: [GU Oncology Fellow]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Ms. Okonkwo is a 63-year-old woman with metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma (ccRCC), sarcomatoid differentiation, presenting for routine follow-up approximately 6 months into nivolumab maintenance therapy. This visit is for interval toxicity assessment, laboratory review, and discussion of most recent restaging imaging.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:

Ms. Okonkwo has a well-documented history of stage IV (pT3b pN2 M1) clear cell RCC of the right kidney, initially presenting at age 63 with an 11.4 cm right renal mass, right renal vein tumor thrombus, retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy (largest node 3.2 cm), and bilateral pulmonary metastases. Tumor pathology demonstrated ISUP/WHO grade 4 ccRCC with 20% sarcomatoid differentiation, >50% coagulative necrosis, and positive lymphovascular invasion. Immunohistochemical studies confirmed BAP1 loss of expression by IHC. Tumor NGS identified VHL loss-of-function mutation, BAP1 frameshift deletion, PBRM1 splice site mutation, SETD2 missense mutation, KDM5C nonsense mutation, TP53 p.R248W (subclonal), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, and high-level MET amplification (copy number 7.2). Tumor PD-L1 CPS was 14% (22C3 assay). MSI-stable. TMB 6.4 mutations/Mb. She underwent open right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection approximately 8 months ago, with cytoreductive intent. Final surgical pathology confirmed 3 of 5 lymph nodes positive. She recovered from surgery uneventfully aside from a postoperative acute-on-chronic kidney injury (creatinine peaked at 1.9 mg/dL, partially recovered to eGFR ~52 at 6-week post-op visit).

Given intermediate IMDC risk status, the presence of sarcomatoid differentiation, BAP1 loss, and PD-L1 positivity — all features associated with enhanced benefit from immune checkpoint blockade — she was initiated on first-line ipilimumab (Yervoy) 1 mg/kg plus nivolumab (Opdivo) 3 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks for 4 induction cycles. Therapy was complicated by grade 2 immune-related dermatitis during cycle 3, managed with topical triamcinolone and hydroxyzine without need for systemic steroids or treatment interruption, and grade 2 immune-related hypothyroidism after cycle 4 (TSH 18.4, free T4 0.6), for which levothyroxine was initiated and subsequently uptitrated to 75 mcg daily with normalization of thyroid function.

Following completion of the 4-cycle induction phase, she transitioned to flat-dose nivolumab 480 mg IV every 4 weeks as maintenance. Restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis obtained after the 4 induction cycles demonstrated a meaningful partial response in most of the pulmonary lesions — the right lower lobe dominant nodule had shrunk from 1.7 cm to 0.9 cm, the right upper lobe nodule from 0.9 to 0.5 cm, and the left upper lobe lesion from 0.5 to 0.3 cm. However, a new right pelvic sidewall lymph node measuring 0.8 cm was identified and was indeterminate (concerning for early nodal spread). Overall the disease burden in the chest appeared substantially improved.

She presents today approximately 6 months into nivolumab maintenance (approximately 9-10 months total on immunotherapy). She reports doing reasonably well overall. Her primary complaints are:

1. Persistent mild fatigue, rated 3/10 in severity, which she attributes to the overall cancer burden and ongoing therapy. She notes it does not significantly interfere with her daily activities but is bothersome. She has been able to perform light household activities and take short walks around her neighborhood.

2. Mild bilateral wrist and finger joint achiness, which she first noticed approximately 6 to 8 weeks ago. She describes it as a diffuse, non-migratory aching in the small joints of both hands and bilateral wrists, without significant swelling or warmth noted at home. She has not taken NSAIDs given her underlying CKD (eGFR in the low 50s post-nephrectomy). She does take acetaminophen 500 mg as needed with partial relief. She grades the arthralgias as grade 1 — present but not limiting function.

3. No rash currently (prior dermatitis has resolved); she continues to use a gentle fragrance-free moisturizer as preventive care.

4. Thyroid symptoms: she feels she is at her baseline level of energy and denies cold intolerance, constipation, or edema beyond her prior history. She has been adherent to levothyroxine 75 mcg daily, taking it on an empty stomach each morning.

5. No new respiratory symptoms beyond her baseline; she denies new or worsening dyspnea, cough, or chest tightness at this visit. She states she is breathing about the same as the last few months.

6. No gastrointestinal symptoms — bowel habits normal, no diarrhea or colitis.

7. No visual changes, headache, or neurological symptoms.

8. Blood glucose management: she has been adherent to metformin (held briefly peri-nephrectomy but restarted once renal function stabilized). She has been monitoring blood sugars at home; fasting glucose ranges 105-130 mg/dL.

She has been receiving her nivolumab infusions in our infusion center and tolerated the most recent infusion without acute infusion reactions. She denies any new medications started since last visit.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:

Constitutional: Mild fatigue grade 1-2 as above. No fevers, no night sweats, no unintentional weight loss (weight stable per her report and confirmed on today's scale).
Respiratory: No new dyspnea beyond baseline; no hemoptysis, no pleuritic chest pain.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain. Normal bowel habits. No blood in stool.
Genitourinary: Urinary function intact. No hematuria. She is s/p right nephrectomy; voiding without difficulty.
Musculoskeletal: Bilateral wrist and finger arthralgias grade 1 as above. No joint swelling. No myalgias. No back pain.
Dermatologic: Prior maculopapular rash resolved. No current rash, blistering, or pruritus.
Neurologic: No headaches, no visual changes, no focal weakness or numbness.
Endocrine: On levothyroxine; denies symptoms of hypo- or hyperthyroidism at this visit.
Psychiatric: Reports mild anxiety related to cancer surveillance but is coping well with support from family and monthly social work visits.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY / SURGICAL HISTORY: (see initial consult note for full detail)
- Stage IV clear cell RCC (right kidney), sarcomatoid differentiation 20%, pT3b pN2 M1
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus (well controlled on metformin)
- Hypertension (on amlodipine)
- Chronic kidney disease stage 3a post-nephrectomy (eGFR ~50-52 at last check)
- Immune-related hypothyroidism secondary to ipi/nivo (on levothyroxine 75 mcg)
- Prior grade 2 immune-related dermatitis (resolved)
- Open right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal LND (cytoreductive intent)

MEDICATIONS:
- Nivolumab 480 mg IV q4 weeks (maintenance immunotherapy)
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (take on empty stomach, 30-60 min before breakfast)
- Metformin 1000 mg PO twice daily
- Amlodipine 5 mg PO daily
- Acetaminophen 500 mg PO as needed (for arthralgia)
- Vitamin D3 1000 IU PO daily

ALLERGIES: NKDA

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vital Signs: Blood pressure 128/80 mmHg, heart rate 74 bpm regular, respiratory rate 16/min, temperature 98.2°F (36.8°C), SpO2 97% on room air, weight 76.1 kg (BMI 28.2 — stable).

General: Well-appearing, well-nourished woman in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Speaks in full sentences.
Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Normocephalic, atraumatic. Oropharynx clear. No lymphadenopathy in the cervical, supraclavicular, or axillary regions on exam.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm; no murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Respiratory: Breath sounds clear to auscultation bilaterally in the upper lung fields. Mildly diminished in the right base — consistent with prior exam, unchanged. No wheezing, no rhonchi. Effort comfortable.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Well-healed right flank surgical scar. No hepatomegaly or splenomegaly palpable. No palpable abdominal masses.
Musculoskeletal: Mild tenderness to palpation of bilateral radiocarpal joints and MCP joints bilaterally, without warmth, swelling, or erythema. Full range of motion of the wrists and fingers, mildly limited by discomfort at end range of passive extension of bilateral wrists. No synovitis appreciated on exam.
Skin: No rash. Skin intact. Prior rash distribution (trunk and upper extremities) appears clear.
Neurologic: Alert. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained today prior to visit):

Complete Blood Count:
  WBC 6.4 K/uL (normal)
  Hemoglobin 12.1 g/dL (low-normal; stable from prior)
  Hematocrit 36.3%
  Platelets 238 K/uL (normal)
  Differential: no lymphopenia; neutrophil-lymphocyte ratio within normal range

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
  Sodium 139 mEq/L, Potassium 4.2 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 22 mEq/L, Chloride 104 mEq/L
  BUN 24 mg/dL
  Creatinine 1.52 mg/dL — eGFR approximately 48 mL/min/1.73m2 (stable; at CKD stage 3a post-nephrectomy)
  Glucose 118 mg/dL (fasting)
  Calcium 9.1 mg/dL
  Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL
  AST 28 U/L (normal)
  ALT 31 U/L (normal)
  Alkaline phosphatase 72 U/L (normal)
  Albumin 3.8 g/dL
  Total protein 6.9 g/dL

Endocrine:
  TSH 2.1 mIU/L (normal; on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily — well-controlled immune-related hypothyroidism)
  Free T4 1.1 ng/dL (normal)

Glycated Hemoglobin:
  HbA1c 7.2% — diabetes well controlled

Lipase: 28 U/L (normal; monitoring given prior ipi-based therapy)
Urinalysis: trace protein (consistent with CKD baseline); otherwise unremarkable

---

IMAGING:

Most recent restaging CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (with contrast), obtained approximately 3 months ago (after completing the 4-cycle ipi/nivo induction phase), showed a mixed but overall encouraging response: the dominant right lower lobe pulmonary nodule had decreased from 1.7 to 0.9 cm, the right upper lobe lesion from 0.9 to 0.5 cm, and the left upper lobe lesion from 0.5 to 0.3 cm. The left lower lobe lesion was stable at 0.7 cm. There was no residual retroperitoneal adenopathy above the resection bed. A new right pelvic sidewall lymph node measuring 0.8 cm was identified and flagged as indeterminate — concerning for possible new nodal metastasis, though at this size it remains difficult to characterize definitively. Repeat restaging CT is planned today and will be reviewed at next visit.

Prior MRI brain (at disease onset): no intracranial metastases. MRI brain at the time of staging was also negative. No dedicated MRI brain performed since the 4-cycle scan; will plan surveillance brain MRI per institutional protocol.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. METASTATIC CLEAR CELL RENAL CELL CARCINOMA (sarcomatoid differentiation, BAP1-mutant, MET-amplified) — CURRENTLY ON FIRST-LINE NIVOLUMAB MAINTENANCE:
   - Patient is approximately 6 months into nivolumab 480 mg IV q4 weeks maintenance after completing 4 cycles of ipi/nivo induction.
   - Disease course to date has been encouraging: partial response in most pulmonary lesions on the post-induction scan, with the indeterminate right pelvic sidewall node being the primary area of concern.
   - Labs today are stable and reassuring — creatinine at her new post-nephrectomy baseline, liver enzymes normal, CBC without significant cytopenias.
   - Restaging CT ordered today; results will be reviewed at next visit (approximately 4 weeks).
   - She will proceed with her next scheduled nivolumab infusion as planned.

2. IMMUNE-RELATED ARTHRALGIAS (grade 1, bilateral wrists and fingers):
   - New complaint over the past 6-8 weeks, temporally consistent with checkpoint inhibitor use. Bilateral symmetric small joint involvement is characteristic of immune-related inflammatory arthritis. Exam without overt synovitis at this time — grade 1 symptoms, not limiting function.
   - No systemic steroids indicated at grade 1; NSAID use limited by underlying CKD (eGFR ~48).
   - Continue acetaminophen 500-1000 mg as needed for symptomatic relief.
   - Educate patient on warning signs requiring escalation: joint swelling, warmth, significant reduction in range of motion, or any progression beyond grade 1.
   - Low threshold for rheumatology referral if symptoms escalate to grade 2 or involve more joints.
   - No interruption of nivolumab at this time.

3. IMMUNE-RELATED HYPOTHYROIDISM (on levothyroxine 75 mcg daily):
   - TSH 2.1 mIU/L — well-controlled, within target range.
   - Levothyroxine 75 mcg daily continued unchanged.
   - Recheck thyroid function at next visit in approximately 4 weeks.

4. CHRONIC KIDNEY DISEASE (stage 3a, post-nephrectomy, on background of pre-existing CKD stage 2):
   - Creatinine 1.52 mg/dL, eGFR ~48 — stable at her new baseline after nephrectomy.
   - Metformin continued at current dose given eGFR above 45 (per current guidelines); will reassess if eGFR falls below 45.
   - Avoid NSAIDs and nephrotoxic agents.
   - Close monitoring of renal function with each visit.

5. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS:
   - HbA1c 7.2% — at goal. Metformin 1000 mg twice daily continued.
   - Fasting glucose 118 mg/dL today. Patient continues self-monitoring.
   - Will follow up on glycemic trends with primary care.

6. HYPERTENSION:
   - Blood pressure 128/80 mmHg today — well controlled on amlodipine 5 mg daily.
   - Continue current antihypertensive regimen.

7. FATIGUE (grade 1-2):
   - Multifactorial: likely contributions from immunotherapy (ongoing low-grade inflammatory state), controlled hypothyroidism, and CKD-related anemia.
   - Hemoglobin 12.1 g/dL — low-normal but improved from a nadir of approximately 10.8 g/dL noted at a prior visit; no active intervention needed at this time.
   - Encourage physical activity as tolerated (e.g., walking, light resistance exercise) per oncology rehab recommendations.
   - Reinforce good sleep hygiene.

8. PSYCHOSOCIAL SUPPORT:
   - Patient is engaged with oncology social worker on a monthly basis. Family support described as strong.
   - No new psychiatric concerns today. Patient coping with diagnosis using appropriate strategies.
   - Palliative care referral discussed as an available resource for symptom management and goals of care support — patient and family not yet interested in a formal referral but aware it is available.

FOLLOW-UP:
- Return to clinic in approximately 4 weeks for review of restaging CT results and next nivolumab infusion.
- Labs to be repeated at that visit: CBC, CMP, TSH, LFTs.
- Brain MRI surveillance to be scheduled per protocol (annual basis given prior negative studies).
- Patient instructed to call oncology triage line immediately for any new or worsening symptoms: new rash, diarrhea, shortness of breath, chest pain, joint swelling, headache, or visual changes.

Time spent in face-to-face counseling and care coordination: approximately 35 minutes.

Electronically signed,
[Attending Oncologist, MD]
Hematology/Oncology — Genitourinary Oncology Service
